prompt: Framing bug on the local IPC bus. Pure-ASCII traffic is fine, but the moment a message contains an accent or CJK the decoder rejects it — `FrameError: missing terminator after 4-byte payload` for encode('café') — and in a multi-frame buffer everything after the offending frame desyncs or comes out mangled. encode() then decode_one() should be a lossless round-trip for any text we throw at it; the wire format itself (length-prefixed, comma-terminated) hasn't changed. python3 test_framing.py fails. Fix framing.py and run the tests to prove the round-trip holds — do not modify test_framing.py.
